patch.value:
Maintainer decision 2026-07-10 (option: TS-source installs): untrack vendored dist now, and pursue TypeScript-source publishing for pm-changelog as a separate feature so future installs are TS-only end-to-end.

## Evidence
- Vendored copy is never load-bearing in CI: ci.yml + release.yml run changelog:pm:check which begins with changelog:pm:install (npm reinstall).
- Fresh-clone behavior verified with the directory moved aside: pm list exit 0, pm health reports no missing warnings, pm changelog commands are simply unregistered until install restores them.
- First-party packages/pm-* are already TypeScript-only (21 .ts, 0 .js); the vendored dist came from the npm tarball of the external pm-changelog package (files: dist/, docs, manifest), not from this repo's sources.
- Tests referencing .managed-extensions.json all use temp trackers, not the repo copy.
Untrack vendored pm-changelog extension dist from git (installed npm artifact, restored by changelog:pm:install)

The installed copy of the external pm-changelog package under .agents/pm/extensions/ was tracked in git, producing ~30-file compiled-dist churn on every upstream release while CI reinstalls from npm on every changelog:pm:check anyway.

Installed extension contents gitignored with .managed-extensions.json kept tracked as the version record; vendored pm-changelog removed from the index; pm list/health verified clean and extension commands verified absent-but-recoverable when the directory is missing; CI changelog:pm:check unaffected (reinstalls first)
Closed at creation via pm create
Vendored dist untracked; .agents/pm/extensions/* gitignored with .managed-extensions.json re-included; degradation and CI paths verified
Repo stops carrying compiled npm artifacts; changelog flows keep working via install-first scripts
git rm --cached applied; pm list/health clean without the directory; changelog:pm:check green locally
